prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "B-Skip 跑姿练习" (B-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the leg-extension phase of a B-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot pushing off the ground; left thigh held at parallel to the floor with knee starting at 90 degrees but the lower leg actively extending forward and downward in a clawing motion (the foot is being whipped down toward the ground); arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; this is the classic B-skip drill where the leg paws the ground; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hamstring group of the extending leg (eccentric loading) and hip flexors and calf muscles. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstrings of the extending leg; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors; "臀大肌 / 小腿" pointing to the glute and calf area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
